procedures.name: Generator pre-flight verification-command checklist
procedures.description: Before submitting a draft contract for Evaluator R1 review, the Generator executes every non-null `verification_command` in the draft literally against the pre-sprint repo state and records actual exit codes and stdout under a Technical Notes `Pre-flight verification` subsection. Closes the Evaluator-catches-contract-bug pattern observed across Sprints 8, 9, 10. AMENDED DEC-0028 (Cycle 3): the pre-flight obligation is dual-anchored. The Generator-side step is retained as aspirational; additionally the EVALUATOR, at R1, affirmatively records that it executed each non-null verification_command against pre-sprint state, and flags any contract lacking a `Pre-flight verification` subsection as an advisory. Cycle 3 evidence (S11/S12/S13) showed the defect-prevention outcome was achieved via Evaluator pre-baselining rather than the Generator-side subsection, which was never surfaced.
steps: For each criterion in the draft contract with a non-null verification_command, execute the command literally against the current pre-sprint repo state.
Record the actual exit code and stdout for each command in a `Pre-flight verification` subsection of the contract's Technical Notes.

For each command whose actual output disagrees with the contract's claimed pre-sprint baseline, either (a) fix the command, (b) update the baseline claim, or (c) explain the divergence in Technical Notes.
Sanity-check each criterion: 'does this command actually verify what the criterion text says it does?' Reject any criterion where the answer is no.
Run this procedure BEFORE writing the final contract draft and submitting for Evaluator R1 review.

EVALUATOR R1 CHECKLIST (DEC-0028): At R1 review, the Evaluator MUST (a) execute each non-null verification_command in the draft against pre-sprint state and record the actual exit code/output in the R1 review, and (b) flag any contract that lacks a Generator-authored `Pre-flight verification` subsection as a non-blocking advisory. This dual-anchors PROC-001 so the procedure describes the active defect-prevention mechanism, not only the aspirational Generator step.

procedures.name: Behavioral-coverage floor justification standard
procedures.description: When the contract's computed behavioral coverage is within 5 percentage points of the 60% floor (i.e., 60-65%), the Technical Notes block MUST include a `Coverage Justification` subsection. Addresses the downward trend toward floor (Sprints 8/9/10 = 66/63/62) observed in cycle 2. AMENDED DEC-0028 (Cycle 3): the `Coverage Justification` heading is MANDATORY VERBATIM when coverage is in [60%,65%], and the static-artifact carve-out now has a codified permission test. Actions HK-0009-UPDATE-S12 recommendation (i). Evidence: 6-sprint trend S8=66/S9=63/S10=62/S11=62/S12=54/S13=66; S11 grazed the band with a non-conformant heading, S12 used the carve-out at 54%.
steps: When behavioral coverage is in the 60-65% band, the Technical Notes block MUST include a `Coverage Justification` subsection answering three questions: (a) Is the sprint's natural behavioral surface limited (non-runnable static artifact)? (b) Has any criterion been reclassified from structural or llm-judge to behavioral to lift coverage, and if so what deterministic execution signal supports the reclassification? (c) Does any criterion use a pre-sprint baseline equal to its post-sprint expected value (preservation check), and if so how is the non-no-op claim defended?
When reclassification happens within a single contract revision cycle (R1 -> R2), the Revision History MUST include a one-paragraph rationale citing the deterministic execution signal.
The Evaluator's R1 review template SHOULD include an explicit check for this subsection when coverage is within the band.

MANDATORY HEADING (DEC-0028): When behavioral coverage is in [60%,65%], the labelled `Coverage Justification` heading is REQUIRED VERBATIM (not an alternative phrasing such as a generic "Behavioral coverage" block). A contract in the band without the exact heading is flagged at R1.

STATIC-ARTIFACT CARVE-OUT PERMISSION TEST (DEC-0028): The carve-out (behavioral coverage permitted below 60%) is allowed ONLY when all three hold: (a) every deliverable is a non-runnable static artifact with no shell entry point; (b) the Technical Notes state the coverage figure BOTH with and without any "simulation behavioral" criterion; (c) a precedent sprint invoking the carve-out is cited (e.g., Sprint 7, Sprint 12). If any condition fails, the carve-out is not permitted and the contract must reach >=60% behavioral.
applicable_to: contract drafting when behavioral coverage in [60%, 65%]
contract revision when reclassifying criteria across grader_type boundaries
contract drafting invoking the static-artifact carve-out below the 60% floor

procedures.name: R2 reviewer literal-execution protocol
procedures.description: When R1 NEEDS REVISION cites a defect in a verification command and the Generator's R2 submission claims a fix, the R2 reviewer MUST execute the literal corrected command against the pre-sprint artifact. Closes the Sprint 10 R2 transcription error pattern (SG5 vacuous-gate finding).
steps: When R1 NEEDS REVISION cites a defect in a verification command, and the Generator's R2 submission claims a fix, execute the literal corrected command against the pre-sprint artifact.
The R2 review block MUST contain a `Verification Results` section with one literal-command quote per resolved issue, capturing exit code and observed output verbatim.
Reasoning about whether a new pattern *should* match -- without execution -- is explicitly insufficient.

When the literal-command count returns 0 from an extraction-bounded command (e.g., `awk '...' | grep -c '...'`), additionally verify the bounding extraction itself matched the intended section (non-empty awk output).
If R2 verification yields a non-expected result, flag the contract NEEDS REVISION (round 3) rather than APPROVED and record the actual observed output.
